steps.title: Set w(r) = 0 and isolate fractions
steps.content:
Starting with $w(r) = \frac{1}{r-5} - \frac{r-4}{-r+4.25} = 0$, we rearrange to isolate the fractions on opposite sides:
$$\frac{1}{r-5} = \frac{r-4}{-r+4.25}$$

steps.title: Simplify the denominator and cross-multiply
steps.content:
Rewrite the right denominator: $-r + 4.25 = -(r - 4.25)$, so:
$$\frac{1}{r-5} = -\frac{r-4}{r-4.25}$$
Cross-multiplying gives:
$$r - 4.25 = -(r-5)(r-4)$$

steps.title: Expand and form a quadratic equation
steps.content:
Expand the right side:
$$r - 4.25 = -(r^2 - 9r + 20)$$
$$r - 4.25 = -r^2 + 9r - 20$$
Rearranging to standard form:
$$r^2 - 8r + 15.75 = 0$$
Multiply by 4 to clear decimals: $4r^2 - 32r + 63 = 0$

steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula and find the greater root
steps.content:
Using the quadratic formula with $a = 4$, $b = -32$, $c = 63$:
$$r = \frac{32 \pm \sqrt{1024 - 1008}}{8} = \frac{32 \pm \sqrt{16}}{8} = \frac{32 \pm 4}{8}$$
This yields $r = \frac{36}{8} = \frac{9}{2}$ or $r = \frac{28}{8} = \frac{7}{2}$.
The greatest value is $\frac{9}{2}$ or $4.5$.

Equivalent forms accepted: 9/2, 4.5.
